steps.title: Establish similarity and find the scale factor
steps.content: Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, triangle $SLK$ is similar to triangle $SRT$ (they share angle $S$, and parallel lines create equal corresponding angles). The ratio of the corresponding parallel sides is: $$\frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{21}{63} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply the similarity ratio to express KT in terms of ST
steps.content: In similar triangles, all corresponding linear measurements scale by the same ratio. Therefore: $$\frac{SK}{ST} = \frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{1}{3}$$ This gives $SK = \frac{1}{3}ST$, so: $$KT = ST - SK = ST - \frac{1}{3}ST = \frac{2}{3}ST$$

steps.title: Find ST using the area formula
steps.content: Since angle $T$ is a right angle, sides $\overline{RT}$ and $\overline{ST}$ are perpendicular, making them the base and height. Substituting the given values into the area formula: $$\frac{1}{2} \cdot 63 \cdot ST = 252$$ $$ST = \frac{504}{63} = 8$$
